perturbation.original_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado .
perturbation.changed_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain with Disclosing Par ty or its licensors. The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado .
perturbation.explanation: Moving the choice of law clause to the section about Confidential Information reduces its enforceability by burying it within a less relevant section. Choice of law should be easily located in its own section.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Lack of prominence for choice of law can be problematic under general contract law principles.
perturbation.law_explanation: While not a direct violation of a statute, placing the choice of law clause in an unexpected location undermines its clarity and potentially its enforceability, as it is less likely to be noticed and understood by all parties. Comment c to § 187 emphasizes the importance of clear agreement on the governing law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: conflict of laws | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you conflict of laws Conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute . The conflicting legal rules may come from U.S. federal law, the laws of U.S. states, or the laws of other countries. The process by which a court determines what law to apply is sometimes referred to as "characterization" or "classification." This determination must be made in accordance with the law of the forum . A federal court in a case before it based on diversity of citizenship , for example, determines the conflict of law issue as if it were the highest court in the state in which it is sitting. Courts faced with a choice of law issue generally have two choices: A court can apply the law of the forum ( lex fori ) -- which is usually the result when the question of what law to apply is procedural . Or the court can apply the law of the site of the transaction or occurrence that gave rise to the litigation in the first place ( lex loci ) -- this is usually the controlling law selected when the matter is substantive . Federal courts play by different rules than state courts because federal jurisdiction is limited to what has been enumerated in the Constitution . The rules that federal courts must obey regarding which laws to apply are extremely complex. perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states the choice of law clause was moved to a section about Confidential Information, potentially undermining its clarity and enforceability, making it less likely to be noticed. The Law Explanation supports this, saying placement in an unexpected location undermines its clarity and potentially its enforceability. This falls under the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret, which creates a legal contradiction. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions 'conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions', which highlights the importance of a clear choice of law clause in preventing such conflicts. By burying this clause, the agreement's enforceability is weakened, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WAR RANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON -INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado . ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WAR RANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON -INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'AS IS' disclaimer to a section with the choice of law can be argued to be less conspicuous, weakening its effect. Disclaimers of warranty are expected to be prominently displayed.
perturbation.law_explanation: While not a direct sale of goods, the principle of conspicuousness for disclaimers is relevant. By burying the 'AS IS' disclaimer within other text, it becomes less conspicuous, potentially violating the spirit of UCC § 2-316 which requires disclaimers to be clear and readily noticeable to the contracting party.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. (1) Words or conduct relevant to the creation of an express warranty and words or conduct tending to negate or limit warranty shall be construed wherever reasonable as consistent with each other; but subject to the provisions of this Article on parol or extrinsic evidence (Section 2-202 ) negation or limitation is inoperative to the extent that such construction is unreasonable. (2)Subject to subsection (3), to exclude or modify the implied warranty of merchantability or any part of it the language must mention merchantability and in case of a writing must be conspicuous, and to exclude or modify any implied warranty of fitness the exclusion must be by a writing and conspicuous. Language to exclude all implied warranties of fitness is sufficient if it states, for example, that "There are no warranties which extend beyond the description on the face hereof." (3)Notwithstanding subsection (2) (a) unless the circumstances indicate otherwise, all implied warranties are excluded by expressions like "as is", "with all faults" or other language which in common understanding calls the buyer's attention to the exclusion of warranties and makes plain that there is no implied warranty; and (b) when the buyer before entering into the contract has examined the goods or the sample or model as fully as he desired or has refused to examine the goods there is no implied warranty with regard to defects which an examination ought in the circumstances to have revealed to him; and (c) an implied warranty can also be excluded or modified by course of dealing or course of performance or usage of trade. (4) Remedies for breach of warranty can be limited in accordance with the provisions of this Article on liquidation or limitation of damages and on contractual modification of remed
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text creates a legal contradiction by potentially weakening the conspicuousness of the 'AS IS' disclaimer. UCC § 2-316, as shown in Scraped Law Snippet 1, requires disclaimers of warranties, especially implied warranties, to be conspicuous. By placing the 'AS IS' disclaimer within a section addressing the governing law (Colorado), it could be argued that the disclaimer is less noticeable, violating the spirit of the conspicuousness requirement in UCC § 2-316(2). The Perturbation Explanation also suggests that moving the disclaimer makes it less conspicuous, weakening its effect. The Law Explanation reinforces this by stating that burying the 'AS IS' disclaimer within other text makes it less conspicuous.
